The Bridge is the front door of KayocktheOS.
The Bridge is not a launcher. It is the operating environment.
The Bridge becomes the center of KayocktheOS.
The browser becomes one tool/room, not the identity of the whole system.

write_text("Foundry/Releases/feature002_bridge_app_notes.md", "# Feature 002 - Bridge App

Adds native Electron Bridge starter.
")

- Added `Bridge/` Electron app starter.

"

LAUNCHER_BAT = '@echo off
title KayocktheOS Workshop Launcher
color 0A
cd /d "%~dp0"

echo ==========================================
echo        KayocktheOS Workshop Launcher
echo ==========================================
echo.
echo Opening the Workshop...
echo.

echo [1/4] Checking Core API...
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -Command "try { Invoke-RestMethod http://127.0.0.1:8844/api/ping -TimeoutSec 2 | Out-Null; exit 0 } catch { exit 1 }" >nul

DESKTOP_NOTE = '# KayocktheOS Launcher Notes

Recommended user-facing launcher:

```text
Z:\KayocktheOS\Launch_KayocktheOS_Workshop.bat
```

This launcher:

1. Checks whether the Core API is already online.
2. Starts the Core if needed.
3. Checks Bridge dependencies.
4. Opens the Bridge.

The goal is to make KayocktheOS feel like one application instead of a set of scripts.
'
This is now the preferred front-door launcher for the project.

" if "features:" in text else "
features:

KayocktheOS should have a single preferred front-door launcher.
The Operator should not need to remember which scripts to start first.
